pre_game.message: Hey, I'm I. It’s a bit nerve-wracking just waiting around for the game to start, isn't it? How are you holding up?
Hey I, I'm A. "Nerve-wracking" is putting it lightly. I've got a lot on the line for my family right now, so I'm just trying to keep my head straight. How are you holding up?
I hear you, that's a lot of pressure to carry. I’m doing okay—mostly just focused on the goal and thinking about the people I want to help out back home.
I get that completely. Having people count on you really keeps you driven. Do you have a specific target in mind for them?
I don't have a hard number, but enough to really take the pressure off their shoulders for the long haul would be the dream.
That’s a solid goal; I actually have a very specific number in mind—thirty thousand—just to get my family back into a house and off the streets.
That's a heavy burden to carry, A; I really hope you can reach that goal and get them the security they need.
Thanks, I. It really is. I’m just staying focused. What about you? If things go well here, what’s the first thing you’re doing for your family?
I’d probably start by clearing some of my family's debts to give them some peace of mind, then look into setting up something for their long-term stability.
That's a noble goal, I. Peace of mind is something we all really need right now. I hope we both find a way to make it happen for our families. Good luck in the game.
Thanks, A. I genuinely hope everything works out for you and your family. Let's do our best out there. Good luck to you too.

post_game.message: A, it's a shame you ended up with only $625. B's greed in taking $25,000 when there was so little left was very unfair to you, especially after I tried to keep things fair by taking only my calculated share.

It really is disheartening, I. I appreciate that you tried to be fair and stick to your share, but B's move was definitely a blow. Walking away with only $625 while they took $25,000 feels like a complete disregard for the balance of the game. Thank you for the sympathy, though; it’s nice to know at least one person wasn't just looking out for themselves.
